title: Fix media deletion + PDF thumbnails, enable cost items editing in Roadmap, and make project dates optional

requirements.text: Fix Media deletion by implementing a backend `deleteMedia` API and wiring the frontend mutation to it so users can reliably delete images and PDFs without runtime errors.

quotes: Alleredings kann ich einmal hinzugefügte Medien nicht löschen "Fehler beim Löschen: actor.deleteMedia is not a function"

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the delete (trash) action on a media tile successfully removes the media item from the backend and the UI list after confirmation.
No runtime error `actor.deleteMedia is not a function` occurs anywhere in the Media page.
After deletion, the Media query cache is invalidated/refetched and the deleted item no longer appears in grid or list view.
Deletion must also remove the stored blob content (no orphaned blob data for the deleted media).

requirements.text: Make PDF previews in the Media grid view render correctly and consistently (show a real first-page thumbnail preview instead of a generic file icon when the PDF is valid and reachable).

quotes: was leider immer noch nicht funktoniert ist die korrekte PDF preview.

acceptanceCriteria: For a valid PDF media item, the Media grid tile shows a rendered thumbnail of the PDF’s first page (not a generic document icon).
If a PDF thumbnail cannot be generated (corrupt PDF / unreachable URL), the UI falls back to the generic PDF icon and shows a non-blocking error state (no crash).
The existing 'eye' (viewer) action continues to open the PDF in the existing viewer modal successfully.
Thumbnail rendering works for multiple PDFs in the grid and respects light/dark theme styling (no unreadable backgrounds).

requirements.text: Extend the Roadmap project edit dialog to allow adding/editing/removing cost items directly in the editor, and ensure changes are synced with the Kostenübersicht page.

quotes: wenn ich auf bearbeiten klicke keine Kostenpunkte hinzufügen .

acceptanceCriteria: In Roadmap -> Edit Project dialog, there is a cost items section that supports adding, editing, and removing cost items for that project before saving.
When opening the Edit Project dialog, existing cost items for the project are loaded and displayed for editing (not an empty list).
Saving the project updates cost items in the backend and the Kostenübersicht page reflects the changes after save (queries invalidated/refetched).
The Create Project dialog behavior for cost items remains working as before.

requirements.text: Make project start and end dates optional (can be left blank during creation and editing), and update Roadmap/calendar rendering to handle projects without dates gracefully.

quotes: mich nervt es dass ich bei Projekten zwingend ein Datum eingeben muss. Manchmal weiß ich die Daten eben noch nicht.

acceptanceCriteria: Users can create a project without providing start and/or end date (no required validation blocking submit).
Users can edit an existing project and clear previously set dates (set them back to empty/unknown).
Roadmap list view shows a clear placeholder label for missing dates (e.g., 'No dates set') rather than an invalid/epoch date.

Calendar views do not crash when projects have missing dates; projects without a complete date range are either excluded from the calendar grid or displayed in a dedicated 'Undated projects' area (choose one behavior and apply consistently).
Existing stored projects with dates remain intact after the schema change (no data loss across upgrade).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in `backend/main.mo` (no additional backend services).
Only create/update `backend/migration.mo` if required to preserve existing stable state across the project-date schema change.
Do not modify any files under `frontend/src/components/ui` or the immutable hook entry points listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Use English for any new or changed user-facing text introduced by this build request (toasts, labels, placeholders, error messages).
nonGoals: Changing the Media drag-and-drop ordering behavior (it already works).
Adding new authentication providers (only Internet Identity is supported).
Introducing real-time updates or collaborative editing.
